content.problemStatement:
Chef has a list of unique integers inputNumbersinputNumbersinputNumbers and wants to explore all possible subsets of this list, including the empty subset. Chef aims to generate the complete power set without any duplicates, regardless of the order in which the subsets appear. 

Help Chef by writing a function that returns all these subsets for the given list of integers.

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindSubsetsfindSubsetsfindSubsets - This function generates all possible subsets (the power set) of a given list of integers.

Parameters
inputNumbersinputNumbersinputNumbers: A reference to an array containing the input integers. The array may be empty and contains distinct integers.
Return Value
Returns an array of arrays representing all unique subsets of the input numbers.
Each inner array is a subset of the input.
The output includes the empty subset and all other subsets in any order.
Constraints
1≤T≤1051 \leq T \leq 10^51≤T≤105
1≤N≤101 \leq N \leq 101≤N≤10
−10≤inputNumbers[i]≤10-10 \leq \text{inputNumbers}[i] \leq 10−10≤inputNumbers[i]≤10
All elements in inputNumbersinputNumbersinputNumbers are unique.

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
For each test case:

The first line contains an integer NNN — the size of Chef's number array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers representing Chef's number array.




Output Format

For each test case, print all subsets of the given array.
Each subset should be printed on a separate line with its elements separated by spaces.
Print the empty subset as an empty brackets.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10) – the size of the array. The second line contains N distinct integers separated by spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case print a block that starts with a line containing '[' and ends with a line containing ']'. Between them print every subset on its own line in the order of increasing binary mask (from 0 to 2^N‑1). Elements inside a subset are printed in the original order, separated by a comma and a space. The empty subset is printed as [] .

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: array [4,7] yields 2^2=4 subsets printed in mask order. Second test case: array [-1,0,1,2] yields 2^4=16 subsets, also printed in mask order.

solution.approach: For each test case we generate all 2^N possible masks. For a mask, we iterate over the N positions; if the i‑th bit is set we append the i‑th array element to the current subset. The subset is printed in the required format. This directly yields the power set without duplicates. The algorithm runs in O(2^N·N) time per test case and uses O(N) auxiliary space for building a single subset.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T·2^N·N) – each of the T test cases enumerates all subsets (2^N) and for each subset scans up to N elements.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) – only the current subset is stored, plus O(1) extra per test case.
